Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Tony Hoare has died 1926 upvotes, 252 comments
  2. Julia Snail – An Emacs Development Environment for Julia Like Clojure's Cider 128 upvotes, 16 comments
  3. U+237C ⍼ Is Azimuth 367 upvotes, 70 comments
  4. TADA: Fast, Reliable Speech Generation Through Text-Acoustic Synchronization 81 upvotes, 21 comments
  5. AI Agent Hacks McKinsey 135 upvotes, 43 comments
  6. Whistleblower: DOGE member took Social Security data to new job 363 upvotes, 135 comments
  7. Cloudflare crawl endpoint 408 upvotes, 154 comments
  8. Faster Asin() Was Hiding in Plain Sight 80 upvotes, 24 comments
  9. Wiz Joins Google 8 upvotes, 0 comments
  10. The entities enabling scientific fraud at scale are large, resilient and growing 105 upvotes, 36 comments
  11. Building a TB-303 from Scratch 160 upvotes, 62 comments
  12. PeppyOS: A simpler alternative to ROS 2 (now with containers support) 48 upvotes, 15 comments
  13. SSH Secret Menu 293 upvotes, 137 comments
  14. Temporal: The 9-Year Journey to Fix Time in JavaScript 16 upvotes, 3 comments
  15. Zig – Type Resolution Redesign and Language Changes 347 upvotes, 182 comments
  16. Microsoft BitNet: 100B Param 1-Bit model for local CPUs 161 upvotes, 87 comments
  17. When the chain becomes the product: Seven years inside a token-funded venture 41 upvotes, 18 comments
  18. Show HN: Klaus – OpenClaw on a VM, batteries included 5 upvotes, 0 comments
  19. Create value for others and don’t worry about the returns 571 upvotes, 392 comments
  20. Making WebAssembly a first-class language on the Web 10 upvotes, 2 comments
  21. Lego's 0.002 mm Specification and Its Implications for Manufacturing (2025) 201 upvotes, 137 comments
  22. Show HN: Open-source browser for AI agents 7 upvotes, 1 comments
  23. Hurricane Electric (HE.NET) IPv6 tunnelbroker page offline due to expired domain 9 upvotes, 2 comments
  24. Yann LeCun raises $1B to build AI that understands the physical world 556 upvotes, 452 comments
  25. Why the Global Elite Gave Up on Spelling and Grammar 11 upvotes, 5 comments
  26. RISC-V Is Sloooow 289 upvotes, 309 comments
  27. Agents that run while I sleep 386 upvotes, 437 comments
  28. Modern wealth is a parlour game played by the well fed 9 upvotes, 4 comments
  29. Ask HN: Is Claude Down Again? 43 upvotes, 38 comments
  30. Let yourself fall down more 15 upvotes, 12 comments

Last generated 12:00am, 12 March 2026. Source here.
